title: Gendos Agenda in NGE and EoE Theory on his master plan
display_title: Gendos Agenda in NGE and EoE Theory on his master plan
wikitext:
[]: Gendo's Agenda in NGE and EoE - Theory on his master plan             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sat Jan 15, 2011 5:29 pm As I was drafting a post in a different thread, an idea developed in my thought concerning Gendo's motives during NGE and EoE. The great Commander Ikari had a master plan concerning Human Instrumentality that went against SEELE's. It involved Rei, Adam and Lilith, and was meant to reunite Gendo with Yui (and probably Shinji too). And it failed. Therefore, we never found out exactly what Gendo was trying to accomplish. I now have a theory about this. Apologies if there have been threads devoted to this topic already, especially if this thought of mine is not new. --- Recall the scene in EoE with Gendo, Adam, Rei III and Lilith, before Rei turned on him. Gendo had grafted Adam into his hand and was grasping inside Rei for something, when she turned on him. My guess is that he was attempting to extract her soul from her failing body, so that he would hold both Adam's and Lilith's souls inside him. He already had Adam's body grafted to his hand (presumably with Kaworu's soul back inside it), and Lilith's body was available within arm's reach. Remember how the ultimate Rei-Being (affectionately called Giant Naked Rei, or GNR) started Human Instrumentality? THIS IS WHAT GENDO WANTED FOR HIMSELF! (Giant Naked Gendo? Eww.) Gendo wanted to control Instrumentality personally, so that he could reunite with Yui again (and Shinji too) in a way that left them together as one forever, with no more need for flesh. He probably had a specific idea about the vessel for their souls, but we can only guess. Possiblities include: - Their souls inhabiting a dormant Unit-01, which then leaves into space, just like Yui did all alone in EoE. - Their souls inhabiting the Black Egg, which then leaves into space. Basically, Human Instrumentality for one small family. - Their souls inhabiting the Ultimate Being, which then leaves into space. Less likely, though, because Adam and Lilith could wrest control of the process from Gendo (like how we saw Kaworu emerge to calm Shinji). Regardless, it would be a merry little family of five, joined as one forever. Yes, I said five: 1. Gendo himself. 2. Yui, the love of his life who had been lost to him for so long. 3. Shinji, the child he has always loved as a concept, but which he could never express. 4. Rei/Lilith, the child he has come to love despite himself, who was named after the daughter Yui and he never had. 5. Kaworu/Adam, to whom Gendo has no known relationship, but who is needed to establish the link. I don't think Gendo spared a thought for the rest of humanity, though. It could well be that he intended to leave earth with Yui and Shinji, leaving the planet to humanity. He would probably defer to Yui or Shinji if they had a preference if humanity should be left alone as separate beings, or as one entity inside the Black Moon / Unit-01 / the MP Evangelions / the LCL ocean from GNG's blood (depending on which of these Gendo intended on keeping for his family). Or he could eject Kaworu/Adam from the chosen vessel of the other souls, and leave earth for Adam to populate with Angels, since neither earth nor the human race hold anything else Gendo wants. (Remember, this is Gendo , after all.) As a final note on this theory, I wish to point out the immense irony of Gendo, who not only represents, but exemplifies the Freudian father-figure, becoming the Ultimate God-like Being to decide the fate of the human race. Gendo as GNG would be very much like the God of the Old Testament: a strict rule-giver to the human race, with harsh teachings and severe punishments. Gendo would be the ultimate patriarchal god, the ultimate father-figure to humanity. And it is only fitting that Rei/Lilith, as the paragon of the Freudian mother-figure to humanity intervened to stop him, and to place the final decision in the hands of Shinji (the Freudian child). --- Thank you for bearing with me.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sat Jan 15, 2011 5:43 pm Good post. Might comment later, but here is a link dump for now: Seele/Gendo's Masterplans: From the Beginning to the End Why did Instrumentality need to be proposed by Gendo? How Gendo's plan differs from SEELE's plan?

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Sat Jan 15, 2011 6:27 pm Nice! I like the comparison with the Freudian mother, father and child figures. What about Fuyutski's support of Gendo? He joined Nerv under duress, yet he is a strong supporter of Gendo's 3I vision (as opposed to Seele's). I think he would defy Gendo if he knew Gendo's plan was just to build a giant spaceship for the Ikari family and whatever happens to the rest of humanity afterward doesn't matter ? Is there a reason your theory needs Adam's soul returned to the embryo before Gendo merges with Rei? I can't think of anything that contradicts this assumption, but there would be a bit less fanwank* involved in your theory if Adam's soul wasn't required. * I'm not using the word "fankwank" in a derogatory sense at all here. Just using the term to describe an assumption to your theory that is not depicted in any canon sources.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sat Jan 15, 2011 7:03 pm nomis1242 wrote: Nice! I like the comparison with the Freudian mother, father and child figures.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jan 16, 2011 5:55 pm As a final note on this theory, I wish to point out the immense irony of Gendo, who not only represents, but exemplifies the Freudian father-figure, becoming the Ultimate God-like Being to decide the fate of the human race. Gendo as GNG would be very much like the God of the Old Testament: a strict rule-giver to the human race, with harsh teachings and severe punishments. Gendo would be the ultimate patriarchal god, the ultimate father-figure to humanity. And it is only fitting that Rei/Lilith, as the paragon of the Freudian mother-figure to humanity intervened to stop him, and to place the final decision in the hands of Shinji (the Freudian child).

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jan 16, 2011 6:36 pm AuraTwilight wrote: How does this follow from Gendo not sparing a thought for humanity and only investing his interests in reuniting with Yui? To be the Freudian father-figure you invision, he'd have to be intensely interested in humanity's affairs in order to enforce a shitload of rules and such. And I doubt you'd find any evidence in the series that Gendo has any of that personality in him whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by chnamanjx » Fri Jun 03, 2011 5:33 am I believe Gendo wanted to save humanity from Instrumentation. He basically spit on Seele's Instrumentation plan towards the end of the series, I don't know the exact quote, but something to the effect of "nothing arises from death". Anyway, my theory is: Gendo is an archeologist / researcher. He humors Seele's religious beliefs in dead sea scrolls so he could work towards uncovering a possible alien life form they found in the Antarctic. 2nd impact (the recovery of Adam) occurs. Gendo and Yui are horrified. They realized the Dead Sea Scroll predictions are real: 1) 2nd impact will cause 17 angels to attack. If any angel succeeds, Humanity will be wiped out in 3rd impact. 2) If humanity survives, it can activate Instrumentation as the 3rd impact (merging all souls into a blob of never-changing bliss). 2nd impact triggers Angels to attack Nerv in an attempt to reunite with the body of Adam (the body on the cross is really Adam's clone, not Lilith). Pieces of this Adam clone are used to create Eva series. The Adam clone is actually bait. Seele wanted to draw out the Angels to defeat them, so Humanity can have it's turn to activate instrumentation. Gendo and Yui plays along as being part of Seele's plan. Both Gendo and Yui believes that merging everyone and destroying individuality is an effective death sentence for the species. However, both continue with researching the creation of the Evas. For now, both Gendo and Seele has a mutual goal in stopping the Angels from succeeding. Yui is absorbed into Eva 01 in an accident. It's discovered that an Evas must absorb a person to function, and that the Evas will only sync with that absorbed person's child. Gendo distances himself from his son. He can't bear to be too attached, as the fate of humanity is in his hands, and he doesn't want familial feelings interfere. Gendo hires Kaji to steal Adam's embryo, the original Adam body. Gendo plans to use Adam's embryo and Rei to trigger instrumentation under his control, after the death of the 17th Angel. Once in control of Instrumentation, Gendo will use it to reunite with his wife. Effectively "wasting" Humanity and Seele's only shot at Instrumentation, and avoiding 3rd impact. However. Rei rebels against Gendo. She snatches the embryo of Adam by cutting off Gendo's hand. She places the choice of Instrumentality with Shinji. Shinji, in a manic depressive state, chooses Instrumentality. Everyone is turned into LCL and merged. At the last second, Shinji changes his mind. Shinji and Asuka are the first to choose to reconstitute themselves. However, the world seems irreversibly destroyed. There seems to be no plant or animal life left. Even if the rest of humanity decides to come back, they would quickly starve to death as everything is destroyed.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jun 03, 2011 10:11 am I think Gendo's plan (Which I think was what happened, but was still eventually foiled in EoTV) would have been to controll instrumentality through Rei sort of like Shinji did and it would have involved just tanging everything and everyone... and that's it, no godlike Composite being formed like in SEELEs plan. Also, it's probably worth noting that while getting his Yui back is indoubtedly his main goal, he has pretty dark views on the world and humanity and seems to seriously believe that Instrumentality is the only way for humity to survive

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jun 03, 2011 3:53 pm However, the world seems irreversibly destroyed. There seems to be no plant or animal life left.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jun 03, 2011 4:20 pm AuraTwilight wrote: There are trees.

[]: Hmm             Quote Post by neko_designer » Sun Jul 10, 2011 7:31 am I always thought that gendo wanted to initiate 3i using Rei, gendo reaching inside Rei merely delivered adam inside Rei's womb. Everything went according to plan, giant Rei and everything im sure was part of gendo's plan, the only thing that failed was that Rei 3 had no love or respect or fear of gendo. Had gendo initiated 3i with Rei 2 (who loved him) she would have granted gendo the power to create a world were he was loved by everyone and were yui lived by his side forever.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jul 10, 2011 3:01 pm Gendo didn't want a world where he was "loved by everyone". He just wanted Yui back, which probably meant he wanted to go inside Unit 01. Rei followed the plan until she ripped Gendo's hand off.
